behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and meaning-preserving (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural translation quality, which is model-dependent and can vary by phrasing/run. A mocked translator cannot reveal semantic/fluency failures, so lower levels would miss real defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of source sentences through the real translation API and score adequacy/fluency against reference translations (or expert rubric), failing if semantic preservation or grammatical quality drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: On primary API 503, service falls back to dictionary translator and still returns success to caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real HTTP/API error handling plus service fallback wiring. Mocking can hide transport/client-layer behavior (status mapping, retries, timeout/error surfacing), so real process+HTTP interaction is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the service against a real/stub HTTP endpoint that actually returns 503, with SQLite fallback configured; assert caller gets 200 with fallback translation and no propagated upstream error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns the correct pre-stored phrase translation from SQLite.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is specifically about module-to-database interaction (querying SQLite and mapping result), not just pure function logic. Unit tests with in-memory fakes can miss SQL/schema/binding issues.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real local SQLite test DB with known phrase pairs, call fallback translator module, and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation plus persistence crosses module boundaries (distance logic + storage). Pure unit checks of distance alone miss serialization/schema/write-path failures.
behaviors.test_description: Submit paired primary/fallback outputs through the quality-tracking path, verify stored DB metric equals expected character edit distance for multiple edge cases (empty strings, unicode, long text).

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is orchestration across components: selection policy + async background quality logging + persistence. No real model-quality judgment is required, so Agentic is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators enabled in test wiring, call service and assert response uses primary output immediately; then await background worker completion and assert quality comparison record was written.
